metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the Approach section's description bullet to replace `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` with `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote` (the method mandated by remediation item 2 and the added AC), so the spec is internally consistent and an executor does not need to resolve the conflict themselves.
evidence: Approach: 'compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`) against the mapper-decoded remote text'
Remediation item 2: 'NOT `sanitize_description`' (explicit prohibition), 'Instead, mirror the canonical differ ... `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote`'
Added AC: 'The description comparison uses `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote` (no warning) ... NOT `sanitize_description`'
findings.finding: The Approach section and the Plan-review remediation section give conflicting instructions for the description comparison method. The Approach section says to use `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` ('compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`)'), while remediation item 2 explicitly overrides this and forbids it ('NOT `sanitize_description`'), specifying `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote` instead. An executor reading the Approach section first could implement the wrong method and only discover the contradiction later in the remediation subsection.
findings.location: Approach section (description bullet) vs Plan-review remediation item 2
scenarios: An executor follows the Approach section verbatim and implements sanitize_description, which then fails the added description AC and emits logger.warning on every reconcile-check pass.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach section, replace: 'compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`) against the mapper-decoded remote text with the same rstrip tolerance' with: 'ADF-fit the local description via `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote({"description": local_desc}, ticket=ticket).get("description")` (no warning) and compare with rstrip tolerance (`_text_matches`) against the mapper-decoded canonical remote description — mirroring `outbound_field_diff.py:270-279`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In remediation item 2 (or the Approach section), state explicitly what `ticket` is in reconcile_check's context — e.g., 'the local Rebar ticket from the bound pair, matching the object type accepted by `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote` in outbound_differ'.
evidence: Remediation item 2: '`OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote({"description": local_desc}, ticket=ticket)`' — `ticket` undefined in reconcile_check context
ABSENCE: no statement mapping `ticket` to a field of the bound pair or defining its type in reconcile_check's call signature
findings.finding: The `ticket` parameter passed to `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote({"description": local_desc}, ticket=ticket)` within reconcile_check is never identified — the plan does not state what object `ticket` refers to in the reconcile_check call context (the local Rebar ticket from the bound pair, or some other object), nor its type. An executor must infer this by reading outbound_differ source code.
scenarios: An executor is unsure whether `ticket` is the local Rebar ticket object from the bound pair, a raw dict, or some adapter-specific object, and may pass the wrong object, causing a runtime error or silent misbehavior.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote` call description: '(where `ticket` is the local Rebar ticket from the currently-checked bound pair, the same object type outbound_differ passes at `outbound_field_diff.py:270`)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Re-parent ad44 from 625b to bbf1 (the epic that explicitly enumerates ad44 as a direct child), OR amend 625b's plan to explicitly authorize and scope the reconcile_check.py canonicalization work — the latter requires re-review of 625b since it expands that story's declared scope.
evidence: Leaf Scope: 'src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile_check.py (canonical comparison; drop vendor sibling loads)', 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py (remove the carve-out; cover reconcile_check.py)', 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ (reconcile-check comparison behavior tests)'

Epic bbf1 child decomposition lists ad44 as a direct open child of the epic: 'Canonicalize reconcile_check.py (ad44-9f46-07aa-480c, depends_on the canonical field diff)' — it is listed as a sibling of 625b, not a descendant.
The leaf itself says '625b' is 'the prerequisite canonical-differ story' (a dependency), not the authorizing parent.
findings.finding: The leaf (ad44) is parented to 625b but its entire scope — canonicalizing reconcile_check.py, removing its by-path sibling loads, and extending the neutrality gate with a reconcile_check carve-out removal — is absent from 625b's plan, scope, testing, and acceptance criteria. 625b covers only outbound_differ.py and related files; reconcile_check.py does not appear in 625b's text at any point. The epic (bbf1) explicitly enumerates ad44 as a direct child of the epic alongside 625b, not beneath it. The leaf is delivering something outside the parent's declared scope, which is a G7 containment violation.
findings.location: Leaf Scope section; parent 625b Scope section; epic bbf1 child decomposition
scenarios: A reviewer checking 625b's completion sees no reconcile_check.py work in scope, so ad44's deliverables have no parent-authorized home — they could be discarded or re-scoped without violating 625b's contract.
The parent (625b) plan-review attestation does not cover reconcile_check.py, so plan-review checks that assume parent coverage of the leaf's scope will miss this entire module.
findings.suggested_fix: Change ad44's parent_id from 625b-9cd1-1ce9-4e5f to bbf1-82e1-cf9d-494a. The epic's own child decomposition already places ad44 as a direct child, so this is a structural correction, not a scope change. 625b remains the prerequisite dependency (add a depends_on link if not already present), but the authorizing parent is bbf1.
